title: Histiopteris vespertilionis
intro: Histiopteris vespertilionis, also known as the flying fern or New Zealand spleenwort, is a type of lycopodiophyte fern native to New Zealand. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in wound healing and the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis. The bioactive constituents of Histiopteris vespertilionis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Histiopteris vespertilionis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory conditions and skin irritations.
taxonomy: Histiopteris vespertilionis is classified as a species of fern in the family Lindsaeaceae. This plant is native to Australia and New Zealand.
